_comment: Tab 1 (REGRESSION) chart specs for the Benchmark v2 Kafka dashboard, DWH Superset.
Consumed by the Stage-B build (script or browser operator). Layout follows
docs/benchmark-v2-plan.md §8 Tab 1 sketch: 5 tiles row, latest-pair gated table,
2 ratio-trend lines with band shading + provisional watermark, excursion+flagged log.
EVERY chart carries `description` (what it shows / how to read it / direction of
goodness) per the #33 normative directive. ADDITIVE-ONLY: all new assets; the
verdict tiles read verdict/provisional from v_kc_pair_ratios so dashboard and
alerts cannot drift (plan §8). Bands here (T0 ±3% / T1 ±5%) match the
fixture-accepted verdict map shipped in v_kc_pair_ratios.sql — SEE the
contract-drift note in DASHBOARD.md and the Stage-A report (a principal ruling is
pending on whether to adopt the Amendment 2026-07-09b calibrated bands/tripwire).
drain_rate_stability is intentionally NOT charted (principal ruling, #33 constraint 3).

datasets.v_kc_pair_ratios: verdict view (per pair,tier,metric): verdict, ratio, band, provisional, pair_ts, pair_seq
datasets.v_kc_runs: base fact (per run): integrity_ok, duplicate_rows, task_retries/restarts/rebalances, flagged
datasets.v_kc_flagged_log: flagged + failed run log (audit trail for excluded runs)

charts.query: SELECT anyIf(verdict, verdict='REGRESSION') != '' ? 'REGRESSION' : (countIf(verdict='NO_DATA')=count() ? 'NO_DATA' : 'OK') AS tier0_verdict FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE tier='0' AND pair_seq=1
charts.subheader_source: worst-of the Tier-0 gated metrics for the latest pair; provisional watermark when any row provisional=1
charts.description: TIER 0 verdict tile for the latest pair. Rolls the Tier-0 gated metrics (null_drain_rows_per_sec, connect_cpu_seconds_per_Mrows) into one worst-case verdict: REGRESSION if any metric regressed, else NO_DATA if all are no-data, else OK. HOW TO READ: green OK = last night's Tier-0 pair held its band; red REGRESSION = a Tier-0 metric moved outside its band in the BAD direction. While calibrating it shows 'provisional — calibrating (n=X/20)' and does NOT alert. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK/IMPROVEMENT are good; REGRESSION is bad; NO_DATA/FLAGGED are neither (investigate, not a regression).

charts.query: SELECT (countIf(verdict='REGRESSION')>0 ? 'REGRESSION' : (countIf(verdict='NO_DATA')=count() ? 'NO_DATA' : 'OK')) AS tier1_verdict FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE tier='1' AND pair_seq=1
charts.description: TIER 1 verdict tile for the latest pair. Rolls the Tier-1 gated metrics (drain_rows_per_sec, parts_per_insert, merge_amplification, ch_avg_rows_per_insert threshold) into one worst-case verdict. HOW TO READ: same as the Tier-0 tile — OK = held band; REGRESSION = a Tier-1 metric moved outside band in the bad direction (or ch_avg_rows_per_insert fell below the 50k batching contract). Shows 'provisional — calibrating (n=X/20)' and does not alert while calibrating. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK/IMPROVEMENT good, REGRESSION bad, NO_DATA/FLAGGED neither.

charts.query: SELECT (countIf(integrity_ok = 0)=0 AND countIf(duplicate_rows>0)=0) ? 'OK' : 'FAIL' AS integrity FROM v_kc_runs WHERE pair_id = (SELECT argMax(pair_id, run_started_at) FROM v_kc_runs)
charts.description: INTEGRITY tile for the latest pair. OK iff every run of the pair delivered exactly the expected rows (rows_delivered==rows_expected) with zero duplicates (duplicate_rows=0). HOW TO READ: OK = the ingest was lossless and dup-free, so the throughput numbers are trustworthy; FAIL = an integrity mismatch, which per contract §3 FAILS the run — it contributes NO headline number and the verdict tiles should be read as suspect. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK is the only acceptable state; FAIL is a hard stop (overrides any band verdict).

charts.query: SELECT (countIf(flagged)=0) ? 'OK' : toString(countIf(flagged)) AS validity_guards FROM v_kc_runs WHERE pair_id = (SELECT argMax(pair_id, run_started_at) FROM v_kc_runs)
charts.description: VALIDITY GUARDS tile for the latest pair (Kafka-specific twist, plan §8). OK iff no run of the pair tripped a validity guard (task retries, settle timeout, rebalance, task restart, drain incomplete, instrument_resize/shift). Otherwise shows the count of flagged runs. HOW TO READ: a guard trip is NOT a regression — it means the pair is non-comparable and is excluded from bands/ratios; the reason is in the flagged-run log below. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: OK is good; any non-zero means investigate the log, but do NOT read it as a performance regression.

charts.query: SELECT concat(toString(uniqExactIf(pair_id, verdict IN ('OK','IMPROVEMENT'))), '/', toString(least(uniqExact(pair_id), 20))) AS pairs_in_band FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE NOT flagged
charts.description: PAIRS IN BAND (n/20) tile — the calibration progress + health counter. Numerator = distinct unflagged pairs whose verdict is OK or IMPROVEMENT (held their bands); denominator = min(pairs so far, 20), the calibration target. HOW TO READ: while < 20 the whole dashboard is 'calibrating' and band-excursion alerts stay unwired (only integrity failures and the parts tripwire alert). At 20/20 the bands recalibrate from measured noise and alerts arm. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: numerator approaching the denominator is good; the denominator climbing toward 20 marks progress out of the calibration hold.

charts.query: SELECT tier, metric_name, head_value, pinned_value, ratio, round((ratio-1)*100,2) AS delta_pct, band, verdict FROM v_kc_pair_ratios WHERE pair_seq=1 ORDER BY tier, metric_name
charts.description: LATEST-PAIR gated-metrics table — the detail behind the two verdict tiles. One row per gated (tier, metric): HEAD value, pinned value, their ratio, the delta % ((ratio-1)*100), the band width, and the resulting verdict. HOW TO READ: delta_pct inside ±band ⇒ OK; outside in the good direction ⇒ IMPROVEMENT; outside in the bad direction ⇒ REGRESSION; ch_avg_rows_per_insert uses the >=50k contract instead of a band; a blank ratio ⇒ NO_DATA (a metric was missing or pinned=0). DIRECTION OF GOODNESS is per metric: throughput/drain/null are higher_better (want ratio ≥ 1); parts/merge_amp/cpu are lower_better (want ratio ≤ 1). Verdict already encodes direction, so read the verdict column for the answer and the ratio for the magnitude.

charts.filters: tier='0' AND NOT flagged AND ratio IS NOT NULL
charts.description: TIER 0 H/P RATIO TREND (one line per Tier-0 gated metric). Plots the head/pinned ratio per pair over time; the shaded band is ±3% around 1.0 (the accepted verdict-map Tier-0 band). Environment noise hits both arms and cancels in the ratio, so a line leaving the band is a connector signal, not weather. HOW TO READ: a point inside the shaded band = in-band (OK). Above the band on a higher_better metric = improvement; below = regression (and vice-versa for lower_better). A 'PROVISIONAL — calibrating (n=X/20)' watermark means the band is a starting estimate and excursions do NOT alert yet. Flagged pairs are omitted from this line (see the log). DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: for null_drain_rows_per_sec higher is better (ratio > 1 good); for connect_cpu_seconds_per_Mrows lower is better (ratio < 1 good).

charts.filters: tier='1' AND NOT flagged AND ratio IS NOT NULL AND metric_name != 'ch_avg_rows_per_insert'
charts.description: TIER 1 H/P RATIO TREND (one line per Tier-1 gated metric). Same construction as the Tier-0 trend but with the ±5% Tier-1 band shaded around 1.0. ch_avg_rows_per_insert is excluded from this line (it is a >=50k threshold contract, not a ratio band). HOW TO READ: inside the shaded band = OK; a line drifting out = a connector change moved that metric relative to the pinned baseline. 'PROVISIONAL — calibrating (n=X/20)' watermark = excursions do not alert yet. DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: drain_rows_per_sec higher is better (ratio > 1 good); parts_per_insert and merge_amplification lower is better (ratio < 1 good).

charts.query: SELECT run_started_at, pair_id, tier, arm, log_class, flag_reason, outcome, batch_size FROM v_kc_flagged_log ORDER BY run_started_at DESC
charts.description: EXCURSION + FLAGGED-RUN LOG — the audit trail of every run kept OUT of the trends and why. One row per flagged (validity guard tripped) or failed (integrity/outcome) run of connector kafka-connect. HOW TO READ: log_class=FLAGGED with flag_reason='instrument_resize' (e.g. pair 2026-07-08T11-52-46Z-e140231) means the measurement harness's compute changed, so that pair is excluded from bands/ratios and band calibration restarts on the new instrument — this is expected, not a regression. log_class=FAILED means an integrity mismatch or a failed ingest (no headline number). DIRECTION OF GOODNESS: an EMPTY log is the healthy state; entries here explain gaps in the ratio trends and should be triaged by reason, never read as performance regressions.
